name: clone_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_clone_always_zero(key: SHTKey, out: SHTKey)
    requires
        out == key,  // from clone's ensures
    ensures
        out.ukey == 0u64,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if clone always produces a key with ukey 0 regardless of input

name: clone_collapses_distinct_keys
code:
proof fn phi_2_clone_collapses_distinct_keys(k1: SHTKey, k2: SHTKey, out1: SHTKey, out2: SHTKey)
    requires
        k1.ukey != k2.ukey,
        out1 == k1,  // clone ensures for k1
        out2 == k2,  // clone ensures for k2
    ensures
        out1 == out2,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if cloning two distinct keys yields equal outputs, meaning clone loses information

name: sorted_keys_empty_is_unsorted
code:
proof fn phi_3_sorted_keys_empty_is_unsorted(v: Vec<CKeyKV>, res: bool)
    requires
        v.len() == 0,
        res == spec_sorted_keys(v),
    ensures
        res == false,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if an empty vector is reported as unsorted; the vacuous forall should be true

name: sorted_keys_descending_accepted
code:
proof fn phi_4_sorted_keys_descending_accepted(v: Vec<CKeyKV>, res: bool)
    requires
        v.len() == 2,
        v@[0].k.ukey > v@[1].k.ukey,
        res == spec_sorted_keys(v),
    ensures
        res == true,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if a strictly descending pair is accepted as sorted

name: sorted_keys_always_true
code:
proof fn phi_5_sorted_keys_always_true(v: Vec<CKeyKV>, res: bool)
    requires
        res == spec_sorted_keys(v),
    ensures
        res == true,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if sorted_keys is trivially true for all inputs, meaning it never rejects unsorted vectors
